results.query: LangGraph state machine architecture for multi-step agents

verdicts.claim: LangGraph orchestrates agent interactions by analyzing, routing, and processing user inputs through a graph-structured workflow, ensuring efficient multi-step task execution
verdicts.reason: Source explicitly states LangGraph orchestrates agent interactions, analyzes, routes, and processes user inputs efficiently.

verdicts.claim: The framework uses a unified state object for dynamic updates and consistency across all nodes, providing precise control over task orchestration
verdicts.reason: Source explicitly states LangGraph maintains unified state object for dynamic updates and consistency

verdicts.claim: LangGraph supports cyclic edges, nodes, and state management, enabling iterative workflows where agents can loop back for correction or refinement rather than executing only in a straight sequence
verdicts.reason: Source explicitly states LangGraph supports complex loops and conditional branches

verdicts.claim: In a machine translation use case, specialized agents (TranslateEnAgent, TranslateFrenchAgent, TranslateJpAgent) were orchestrated via LangGraph, demonstrating improved multilingual accuracy and scalability
verdicts.reason: Source explicitly describes specialized agents (TranslateEnAgent, TranslateFrenchAgent, TranslateJpAgent) orchestrated via LangGraph demonstrating improved multilingual accuracy and scalability.

verdicts.claim: For automated bug fixing, a four-step iterative workflow (Code Generation, Code Execution, Code Repair, Code Update) was implemented using LangGraph combined with a ChromaDB vector database for context-aware, memory-driven debugging
verdicts.reason: Source explicitly describes four-step workflow with LangGraph and ChromaDB for bug fixing

verdicts.claim: LangGraph integrates with Apache Spark to support big data ML workflows, enabling drag-and-drop visual workflow design that is converted into Spark-compatible code for high-performance execution
verdicts.reason: Source explicitly states LangGraph enables visual workflow design converted to Spark-compatible code

verdicts.claim: LangGraph-Go, a Go-native implementation, provides type-safe state management via Go generics, deterministic replay via checkpoints, and a unified ChatModel interface supporting OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, AWS Bedrock, and Ollama
verdicts.reason: Source explicitly lists all claimed features and LLM providers.

verdicts.claim: LangGraph-Go includes production-oriented features such as AWS Bedrock multi-region failover, in-memory persistence (MemStore), and functional options for engine configuration, though it remains in alpha with an unstable API
verdicts.reason: Source explicitly mentions AWS Bedrock multi-region failover, MemStore, functional options, and alpha status with unstable API.
